satire_attempt: Local engineering principal informs paralyzed staff engineer that their migration is 'tasmād yudhyasva.' Engineer, unfortunately, is not paralyzed by a migration that genuinely needs to happen but by their fourth Rust-rewrite proposal this quarter. Principal kicks them out of the conference room. Industry insiders predict the term 'svadharma rewrite' will appear on RFC titles by 2027.
verdict_reason: Mocking the verse requires either (a) inventing the claim that engineers should quote Sanskrit during migrations — which the analog does not claim — or (b) inventing the claim that the verse blesses any rewrite — which the analog explicitly forecloses with the counter-example ('vanity migrations, framework chasing'). My satire above accidentally proves the verse's point: it depicts the verse correctly identifying a vanity-Rust rewrite as NOT licensed. The verse's coupling of action-license to genuine forcing function is exactly the thing my satire cannot dismiss. PASS.
